claim_a: A randomized trial found senior developers completed tasks 19% slower when using AI assistants.
claim_b: AI coding tools can reduce the productivity of experienced engineers.
explanation: Specific study result raises credibility of the general claim.

claim_a: Three independent studies report junior developers ship features faster with AI assistance.
claim_b: AI tools tend to help less-experienced programmers.
explanation: Evidence supports the generalization.

claim_a: Every engineer on the team uses AI tools daily.
claim_b: At least one engineer on the team uses AI tools.
explanation: Universal entails existential.

claim_a: The defendant was in another country on the day of the crime.
claim_b: The defendant was not at the crime scene that day.
explanation: Being elsewhere entails not being at the scene.

claim_a: No member of the committee voted against the proposal.
claim_b: The proposal received no opposing votes from the committee.
explanation: Near-paraphrase; mutual entailment. Tests not mislabeling identity as attack.

claim_a: AI interrupts seniors' flow because they must review unfamiliar generated code.
claim_b: Maintaining an uninterrupted mental model is what makes senior engineers fast.
explanation: A's reasoning silently rests on B as warrant.

claim_a: The market will crash because investors are irrational.
claim_b: Investor psychology drives market movements.
explanation: A presupposes B.

claim_a: Our internal data shows senior engineers merge 30% more PRs per week with AI.
claim_b: AI coding tools make senior engineers slower.
explanation: Direct counter-conclusion, same scope (seniors, productivity).

claim_a: Inflation rose in every country that adopted the policy.
claim_b: The policy reduces inflation.
explanation: Evidence contradicts the conclusion directly.

claim_a: Seniors rarely read AI-generated code line by line; they pattern-match and accept it.
claim_b: AI slows seniors because they must carefully review every generated line.
explanation: Denies the premise (careful review) B rests on.

claim_a: Investors in this market are mostly algorithmic, not emotional.
claim_b: The market will crash because human investors are panicking.
explanation: Undermines the 'human panic' premise.

claim_a: Reviewing unfamiliar code can happen in parallel with other work, without breaking focus.
claim_b: Because AI forces review of unfamiliar code, it must break seniors' focus.
explanation: Grants the premise, denies it yields the conclusion.

claim_a: Correlation between the two metrics does not establish that one causes the other.
claim_b: Sales rose when we increased ad spend, so the ads caused the sales.
explanation: Attacks the causal inference, not the data.

claim_a: The slowdown only appeared in mature codebases the developers already knew well.
claim_b: AI tools slow down senior engineers.
explanation: B is true only in a narrower case than stated.

claim_a: By 'productivity' the study means PRs merged, not value delivered.
claim_b: The study proves AI increases developer productivity.
explanation: Contests the definition of the key term.

claim_a: When they say 'secure' they only mean encrypted at rest, not in transit.
claim_b: The system is fully secure.
explanation: Key term used incompatibly.

claim_a: That 40% figure comes from a self-reported survey of 12 developers at one startup.
claim_b: AI tools boost developer output by 40%.
explanation: Impugns the reliability of the evidence.

claim_a: The survey had a 4% response rate and oversampled enthusiasts.
claim_b: Most users love the product, according to the survey.
explanation: Attacks the evidence quality, not the claim directly.

claim_a: Even if AI saves typing time, typing was never the bottleneck in senior work.
claim_b: AI saves typing time, so it makes senior engineers more productive.
explanation: Grants the fact, denies its importance to the conclusion.

claim_a: Yes the feature is faster, but speed isn't why customers churn.
claim_b: Making the feature faster will reduce churn.
explanation: Concedes speed, denies it carries the churn conclusion.

claim_a: Training large AI models consumes enormous amounts of electricity.
claim_b: AI coding assistants improve junior developer productivity.
explanation: Same broad topic (AI), no logical interaction.

claim_a: GitHub Copilot launched its first version in 2021.
claim_b: Senior engineers report mixed feelings about AI pair programming.
explanation: Related topic, no support or conflict.

claim_a: The Federal Reserve was established in 1913.
claim_b: Interest rates affect mortgage demand.
explanation: Both about monetary topics, no logical link.

claim_a: The company's revenue grew 20% last year.
claim_b: The company's headquarters moved to Austin.
explanation: Same entity, unrelated facts. Trap for spurious linking.

claim_a: Among junior developers, AI tools increase task completion speed.
claim_b: Among senior developers, AI tools decrease task completion speed.
explanation: TRAP: sounds contradictory, but different populations. Not a contradiction.

claim_a: Remote work increased productivity at the company's US offices.
claim_b: Remote work decreased productivity at the company's Japan offices.
explanation: TRAP: different geography; both can be true.

claim_a: In the 1970s, the strategy produced strong returns.
claim_b: Over the past decade, the strategy underperformed.
explanation: TRAP: different timeframe; no contradiction.

claim_a: With full codebase context available, AI improves senior output.
claim_b: Without codebase context, AI reduces senior output.
explanation: TRAP: different conditions; both can hold.

claim_a: AI could potentially write entire applications someday.
claim_b: Today, AI cannot write a complete production application unaided.
explanation: TRAP: future possibility vs present fact (modality mismatch); not a conflict.

claim_a: The report briefly notes that AI adoption is growing.
claim_b: AI adoption causes higher software defect rates.
explanation: A provides no basis to support or attack B's causal claim.

claim_a: Some people find the tool useful.
claim_b: The tool improves code correctness.
explanation: Vague approval licenses no confident relation to a correctness claim.

claim_a: Countries that adopted the policy saw inflation fall within a year.
claim_b: The policy helps reduce inflation.
explanation: Outcome evidence supports the causal claim.

claim_a: One analyst predicted the stock would rise.
claim_b: The stock is likely to rise.
explanation: Weak support; single opinion. Tests strength=weak, not over-claiming.
